brand.name: DeshRide
brand.one_liner: A simulated product demo built from the final FINE 691 pitch deck and executive brief.
brand.hero_title: A safer way to book intercity spare seats in Bangladesh
brand.hero_body: DeshRide combines hub-to-hub booking, prepaid commitment, verified identity, and a bKash-first payment layer so intercity carpooling feels reliable enough to use again.
brand.demo_notice: This is a public presentation demo. Routes, booking steps, payments, and trust flows are simulated for storytelling and do not process real transactions.

overview.problem_summary: Intercity travel in Bangladesh is either affordable but rigid, or flexible but weak on trust. The gap is not mobile adoption. The gap is that no trusted, prepaid intercity carpool marketplace exists today.
problem_points: Intercity buses are affordable, but crowded, schedule-bound, and low on recourse when things go wrong.
Informal ride-sharing already happens through personal networks and Facebook groups, but it is still cash-only, unverified, and unsafe.
Offline leakage is normal in the market, which weakens safety, reliability, and platform accountability.
opportunity_points: Bangladesh already has a large mobile-first population and strong comfort with digital payments.
bKash gives DeshRide a trusted payments rail without having to invent a new wallet.
A corridor-first launch lets trust data and route density compound where behavior repeats.

market_metrics.label: Mobile subscriptions
market_metrics.note: A wide mobile base makes app discovery and repeat use realistic.

market_metrics.label: bKash users
market_metrics.note: Payments can ride on behavior people already trust.

market_metrics.label: Median age
market_metrics.note: A young population supports mobile-first travel coordination.

market_metrics.label: Gen Z population
market_metrics.note: The pilot wedge is comfortable with digital products and flexible travel.

market_metrics.label: Rides happen offline
market_metrics.note: Leakage creates the trust and safety problem DeshRide is designed to solve.

market_metrics.label: BRTA-licensed operators
market_metrics.note: The market is active, but no trusted prepaid intercity carpool leader exists.

routes.name: Dhaka-Chattogram
routes.highlight: Anchor corridor with student, work, and repeat family demand.
why_choose: Faster coordination than informal ride matching
Verified Safe Hub pickup and dropoff

routes.name: Dhaka-Sylhet
routes.highlight: Strong fit for repeat student, work, and family travel.
why_choose: Reliable scheduling on a repeat corridor
Trust-focused booking instead of cash handoff
Easy return-trip behavior for frequent riders

routes.name: Dhaka-Cox's Bazar
routes.highlight: A leisure route where comfort and coordination matter.
why_choose: Higher comfort for long-distance leisure travel
Hub-to-hub pickup keeps the trip predictable

payment_rails.name: bKash
payment_rails.label: Primary rail
payment_rails.detail: The core launch rail because users already trust it for everyday transactions.

payment_rails.name: Nagad
payment_rails.label: Secondary rail
payment_rails.detail: Government-backed and growing quickly, giving the platform a second familiar option.

payment_rails.name: Card
payment_rails.label: Tertiary rail
payment_rails.detail: Useful for card-holding riders and future broader checkout flexibility.

steps.title: Pick a corridor and trip date
steps.body: Riders start with a dense corridor, a date, and a seat count. The product stays focused on repeat intercity behavior rather than open-ended routing.
steps.assurance: The corridor-first design helps DeshRide build route liquidity instead of spreading too wide.

steps.title: Prepay before the trip starts
steps.body: The rider confirms the booking on-platform using bKash first, with Nagad and cards available as supporting rails.
steps.assurance: Prepaid commitment reduces no-shows and gives the platform a clean dispute and payout flow.

steps.title: Meet at a verified Safe Hub
steps.body: Pickup and dropoff happen at named public locations near transport nodes. Riders handle the last mile, but the trade-off improves safety and coordination.
steps.assurance: Safe Hubs turn the trip into a repeatable, lower-friction operating model.

steps.title: Travel with trust tools active
steps.body: Verified identity, trip sharing, and structured support turn a casual match into a more accountable transaction.
steps.assurance: Trust data compounds as the same corridor is used again and again.

steps.title: Rate the trip and close the payout cycle
steps.body: After the ride, both sides rate the experience. The 24-hour dispute window protects riders before the driver receives T+1 payout.
steps.assurance: Ratings and completed-trip history strengthen the Reliability Score over time.

why_it_works.title: Why this platform compounds
why_it_works.subtitle: Density, trust data, and anti-leakage reinforce each other on every corridor.
flywheel: More drivers improve route coverage and timing flexibility.
Better coverage attracts more riders on the same corridor.
More riders improve fill rates and driver earnings.
Stronger earnings encourage more verified drivers to list.

credits.headline: DeshRide Credits keep riders on-platform
points: Riders earn Tk10-15 in credits per completed trip.
Credits work as partial payment on future rides.
Those benefits disappear when bookings move off-platform.
Reliable drivers unlock fuel and maintenance perks as trust builds.

why_it_works.moat_quote: The moat is not the app alone. It is the density of verified drivers on each corridor, the depth of trust data, and the credits riders have accumulated.

comparison_rows.economic_logic: Corridor density, trust data, prepaid commitment
comparison_rows.what_is_different: Built for repeat intercity travel instead of instant local rides or scheduled bus inventory

comparison_rows.economic_logic: Fast local matching and short-haul convenience
comparison_rows.what_is_different: Optimized for immediate door-to-door mobility, not corridor depth
Sell scheduled bus seats rather than trusted private spare capacity

trust_flow.title: Why this is a fintech business
trust_flow.subtitle: The real value is not inventing a new payment rail. It is coordinating payment, settlement, verification, disputes, and future finance layers on top of trusted rails.
payment_steps: Rider prepays via the selected rail
Platform holds funds in an escrow-style flow
Trip completes with trust and safety tools active
24-hour dispute window stays open after the trip

items: DeshRide Credits as a loyalty currency
Driver cash-flow advances backed by verified trip history
Vehicle upgrade financing for top drivers

economics.valuation_note: Seed-stage valuation is framed around milestone progress, market potential, and comparable deals rather than a precise DCF.

launch.title: Launch plan, moat, and the ask

phases.headline: Prove the corridor
bullets: Driver verification and Safe Hub setup

phases.headline: Build the moat
bullets: Launch DeshRide Credits to reduce leakage
Add last-mile bundles and DeshRide Connect

phases.headline: Monetize the data
bullets: Expand to 10 corridors with route dominance
Offer driver finance, fuel, and vehicle credit partnerships
Layer partner monetization on top of trust data
Target: 2,400 daily seats with positive contribution

incumbent_note: Uber and Pathao optimize intracity immediacy, not corridor density.
Shohoz and Jatri sell ticket inventory, not trusted private spare capacity.
Different product, different moat, different economics.
